projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: The consistent presence of 13–16 mobile devices alongside zero camera motion on a weekday afternoon suggests unaccounted activity in non-camera zones or unexpected guests, as this significantly exceeds the household baseline and contradicts typical Wednesday school/work routines.

The gradual decline in connected devices (19→13) alongside zero camera motion on a Wednesday afternoon strongly indicates the house is unoccupied, with the elevated count likely stemming from smart home hubs or background network artifacts rather than occupants. Steve should verify network status to rule out phantom connections.

The persistent 16-20 connected mobile devices combined with zero camera activity on a Wednesday morning strongly suggests either unregistered guests, a network misclassification of stationary IoT devices, or a potential security breach, warranting immediate verification.

The persistent 17-20 connected devices far exceeds the expected household baseline, yet cameras show zero activity. This discrepancy suggests either numerous IoT devices are actively connected or visitors are present in non-camera zones, warranting verification given the weekday morning timing.

The steady increase in connected mobile devices from 16 to 20 between 08:25 and 08:44 suggests multiple new devices are joining the network, which is unusually high for the household and likely indicates unexpected guests. This warrants a quick check to confirm if visitors are expected or if there’s a connectivity anomaly.

The persistent 12–17 device count paired with zero camera motion confirms the household is settled in non-camera zones (likely bedrooms) for the evening, with Roxanne probably resting in bed due to her fragmented sleep. This is a normal wind-down pattern, but her sleep trend warrants monitoring.

The stable 12–16 device count paired with zero camera motion over four hours, alongside Roxanne’s sleep tracking, confirms a quiet, settled household evening with everyone distributed across rooms outside camera coverage. This is a normal post-dinner downtime pattern and requires no action.
Roxanne slept only 2.33h last night. May be a rough morning — heads up.

The persistent 11–15 device count combined with zero camera motion for four hours strongly indicates either a camera system outage, a presence-tracking anomaly, or that the home is actually empty, leaving it unmonitored during a typical weekday afternoon.

Wi-Fi confirms ~12-15 devices are home, yet cameras have logged zero motion or events for four hours, strongly suggesting they are offline, disabled, or in privacy mode rather than the house being still.
